message:
docs(doctor): document --fix and --dry-run modes (bd-31ps.6.4)

Adds comprehensive documentation for the new doctor --fix feature:
- Usage examples for --fix and --dry-run
- Table of safe auto-fixers with descriptions and undo strategies
- Safety guarantees section
- Example dry-run output
- Manual-only fixes explanation
- Undo command documentation

Also closes bd-31ps.6 (parent feature bead) since all children are complete.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -891,7 +891,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-31ps.1.1","title":"Manifest web metadata: schema + types + validation","description":"## Goal
Extend manifest schema + types to include web-facing metadata needed for generated website content.

## Tasks
- Define `web` metadata fields in Module (e.g., display_name, short_desc, docs_url override, category_label, tldr_snippet, command_example, lesson_slug, visibility flags).
- Update packages/manifest/src/types.ts and schema.ts with validation rules (string lengths, optional fields).
- Update parser/validator to surface warnings for missing recommended web fields on first-class tools.
- Document defaults and migration strategy (if web fields omitted, fall back to existing content).

## Considerations
- Keep fields optional to avoid breaking existing manifest entries.
- Avoid overfitting: start with minimal set needed for current pages.
- Ensure schema constraints prevent unsafe content (no HTML injection).

## Tests
- Unit tests for schema validation (valid/invalid).
- Snapshot tests for parse + validate warnings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema accepts existing manifest with no changes.
- New web metadata fields validate correctly.
- Warnings emitted for missing recommended fields on critical modules.","notes":"Implemented ModuleWebMetadata: types.ts interface (20 optional fields), schema.ts Zod validation (hex color, kebab-case icon, href path regex, bounded lengths), parser.ts warnings for web-visible modules missing recommended fields, full exports in index.ts. 170 tests pass. Backwards compatible.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:19:52.551265569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:29:56.695662534Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T22:29:56.695596660Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:19:52.551265569Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.1.2","title":"Generator: emit web data modules","description":"## Goal
Generate web-facing data modules from acfs.manifest.yaml.

## Tasks
- Extend packages/manifest/src/generate.ts to emit apps/web/lib/generated/*.ts (tools, tldr, commands, lessons index).
- Define stable output contracts: types + naming + export shape.
- Ensure generated files include a header warning and are deterministic (sorted output).
- Update generator tests to assert output stability and to cover new data files.

## Considerations
- Keep generated output small and tree-shakable.
- Prefer pure data (no React imports).
- Keep cross-repo path resolution robust in generator (uses repo root).

## Tests
- generate.test.ts: new fixtures for web outputs.
- generate:diff shows expected changes only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running `bun run generate` creates/updates web generated files.
- Output is deterministic across runs.
- No manual edits needed in generated files.","notes":"Completed: Generator emits 5 web TypeScript modules (manifest-tools.ts, manifest-commands.ts, manifest-tldr.ts, manifest-lessons-index.ts, manifest-web-index.ts barrel). All outputs sorted by module ID for determinism, include TS_HEADER warning. 201 tests pass (31 new web data tests). Type-check clean. Output goes to apps/web/lib/generated/.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:20:18.485787766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:37:25.060124891Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T22:37:25.060058907Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:20:18.485787766Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:39:34.487137375Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.1.3","title":"Web app: consume generated tool/lesson data","description":"## Goal
Refactor apps/web to consume generated data modules instead of hand-maintained lists.

## Tasks
- Replace manual arrays in flywheel.ts, tldr-content.ts, commands.ts, lessons.ts with generated imports and small adapters if needed.
- Ensure categories, labels, and ordering match current UI expectations.
- Provide fallback for any missing web metadata (e.g., use module description).

## Considerations
- Avoid breaking existing routes and tests.
- Keep component props unchanged where possible.
- Ensure tree-shaking works and no circular imports.

## Tests
- Web type-check and lint.
- Playwright smoke on /tldr, /learn, /flywheel pages.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No direct edits to generated files.
- UI renders same or better content with generated data.
- Build succeeds with no regressions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:20:40.917316830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:59:09.655423674Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T22:59:09.655404547Z","close_reason":"Web app consumes manifest data: adapter layer, flywheel/tldr/commands merge, build+typecheck pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:20:40.917316830Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:39:56.233300772Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-31ps.1.5","title":"Docs: manifest-driven web generation workflow","description":"## Goal
Document the manifest→web generation workflow for maintainers.

## Tasks
- Update maintainer docs (docs/MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VNEXT.md or new docs) to describe web metadata fields.

 {"id":"bd-31ps.10","title":"Web tool status page (manifest-generated)","description":"## Background
Users want a canonical “what is installed” page that always matches the manifest.

Add a web tool-status page generated from manifest data (categories, verify commands, optional status).

- Include quick filters/search.

## Testing
- Web E2E smoke for the page.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Page renders all tools with categories and descriptions.

Define the generated data needed for the tool status page.

- Add any new fields to manifest web metadata if necessary.

 {"id":"bd-31ps.5.2","title":"Doctor: per-module fix suggestions","description":"## Goal
Provide actionable fix suggestions for failed doctor checks.

## Tasks
- Map module IDs to fix commands (acfs install --only <module>) and optional context notes.
- Display suggestions for missing modules and for optional tools (warn vs fail).
- Ensure suggestions respect installed mode (vibe/safe) and ref pin when known.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Doctor output includes fix hints for missing modules.
- Suggestions are concise and copy-pasteable.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:28:05.778645801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:58:48.436496627Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:28:05.778645801Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-31ps.5.4","title":"Docs: doctor checks derived from manifest","description":"## Goal
Document that doctor checks are manifest-derived and how to fix failures.

## Tasks
- Update README troubleshooting to reference acfs install --only for missing modules.
- Add note that doctor uses generated verify commands.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users understand why doctor checks match manifest and how to recover.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:28:42.675656669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:28:42.675656669Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:28:42.675656669Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-31ps.6","title":"acfs doctor --fix (safe auto-fixes)","description":"## Background
Doctor output is actionable but still manual. Many fixes are deterministic and safe (path order, missing symlinks/config copies).

## Goal
Add `acfs doctor --fix` to apply safe, deterministic fixes with logging, dry-run support, and strong safety guarantees.

## Scope
- Define a whitelist of safe fixers (path order, missing ~/.acfs config copies, dcg hook install prompt, missing symlinks).
- Support --dry-run (preview without changes) and --yes (non-interactive).
- Output a summary of changes made.
- Log all remediation actions to ~/.local/share/acfs/doctor.log

## Safety Constraints (CRITICAL)
- NEVER delete user files or directories
- NEVER modify ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc without creating backup first
- Always confirm before system-level changes (unless --yes passed)
- Each fixer must be idempotent (safe to run multiple times)
- Log all actions for audit trail
- Falls back to manual instructions for unsafe/complex operations

## Fixer Categories
1. **Safe (auto-apply with --yes):**
   - PATH ordering fixes (prepend ~/.local/bin)
   - Missing ~/.acfs config file copies
   - DCG hook install prompt
   - Missing tool symlinks

2. **Prompt required (even with --yes in interactive):**
   - Shell rc file modifications
   - System-wide changes

3. **Manual only (never auto-fix):**
   - Package manager operations
   - Anything requiring sudo
   - File deletions

## Considerations
- Never run destructive operations
- Must respect non-interactive environments (CI/scripts)
- Rollback strategy: create backups before modifying any user file

## Testing
- Unit tests for each fixer function
- E2E test: break tool, run doctor --fix, verify fixed
- Test --dry-run shows correct actions without applying
- Test unsafe operations fall back to manual instructions
- VM test to verify fixes applied correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- Safe fixes apply with clear logs and no surprises
- Dry-run outputs exact actions without applying
- All changes logged to ~/.local/share/acfs/doctor.log
- Unsafe operations gracefully degrade to suggestions","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:29:00.000890342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:38:50.331025442Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.6","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:29:00.000890342Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-31ps.6","title":"acfs doctor --fix (safe auto-fixes)","description":"## Background
Doctor output is actionable but still manual. Many fixes are deterministic and safe (path order, missing symlinks/config copies).

## Goal
Add `acfs doctor --fix` to apply safe, deterministic fixes with logging, dry-run support, and strong safety guarantees.

## Scope
- Define a whitelist of safe fixers (path order, missing ~/.acfs config copies, dcg hook install prompt, missing symlinks).
- Support --dry-run (preview without changes) and --yes (non-interactive).
- Output a summary of changes made.
- Log all remediation actions to ~/.local/share/acfs/doctor.log

## Safety Constraints (CRITICAL)
- NEVER delete user files or directories
- NEVER modify ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc without creating backup first
- Always confirm before system-level changes (unless --yes passed)
- Each fixer must be idempotent (safe to run multiple times)
- Log all actions for audit trail
- Falls back to manual instructions for unsafe/complex operations

## Fixer Categories
1. **Safe (auto-apply with --yes):**
   - PATH ordering fixes (prepend ~/.local/bin)
   - Missing ~/.acfs config file copies
   - DCG hook install prompt
   - Missing tool symlinks

2. **Prompt required (even with --yes in interactive):**
   - Shell rc file modifications
   - System-wide changes

3. **Manual only (never auto-fix):**
   - Package manager operations
   - Anything requiring sudo
   - File deletions

## Considerations
- Never run destructive operations
- Must respect non-interactive environments (CI/scripts)
- Rollback strategy: create backups before modifying any user file

## Testing
- Unit tests for each fixer function
- E2E test: break tool, run doctor --fix, verify fixed
- Test --dry-run shows correct actions without applying
- Test unsafe operations fall back to manual instructions
- VM test to verify fixes applied correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- Safe fixes apply with clear logs and no surprises
- Dry-run outputs exact actions without applying
- All changes logged to ~/.local/share/acfs/doctor.log
- Unsafe operations gracefully degrade to suggestions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:29:00.000890342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:31:29.246418721Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T23:31:29.246397621Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.6","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:29:00.000890342Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.6.1","title":"Doctor --fix: safe fixer spec","description":"## Goal

## Tasks

- Define guard conditions + rollback strategy if any.
- Add a dry-run report format.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clear spec for what --fix will and will not change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:29:17.448072424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:19:04.886508694Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T23:19:04.886490119Z","close_reason":"Complete: Spec document created at scripts/lib/doctor_fix_spec.md covering 6 safe fixers, guard conditions, rollback strategy, dry-run format, CLI interface, and safety invariants","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:29:17.448072424Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-31ps.7","title":"Preflight network + repo health checks","description":"## Background
Install failures often stem from network/DNS/apt mirror issues or low disk space. Catching this early improves success rate.

## Goal
Add preflight checks for network reachability (DNS, GitHub, apt) and system health (disk/memory).

## Scope
- Extend scripts/preflight.sh and/or install.sh preflight phase.
- Provide actionable fix hints.

## Testing
- Unit tests for check functions.
- VM test with simulated failures.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Preflight fails fast with clear remediation steps.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:31:00.046521225Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:31:00.046521225Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.7","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:31:00.046521225Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-31ps.7.1","title":"Preflight checks: DNS, GitHub, apt, disk","description":"## Goal
Implement preflight checks for DNS, GitHub, apt mirrors, and disk space.

## Tasks
- Add DNS resolution test (e.g., github.com, archive.ubuntu.com).
- Add HTTPS reachability test with short timeouts.
- Check disk free space vs minimum threshold.
- Add CPU/memory sanity checks (warn).
- Emit fix hints (e.g., /etc/resolv.conf, apt update).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Preflight catches common failure modes early with clear messages.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:31:46.325624492Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:25:35.976317040Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:31:46.325624492Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-31ps.7.1","title":"Preflight checks: DNS, GitHub, apt, disk","description":"## Goal
Implement preflight checks for DNS, GitHub, apt mirrors, and disk space.

## Tasks
- Add DNS resolution test (e.g., github.com, archive.ubuntu.com).

 {"id":"bd-31ps.8","title":"Pinned ref UX (installer flag + wizard toggle)","description":"## Background
Reproducible installs are safer, but beginners rarely pin refs. A simple UX can make pinning the default when desired.

## Goal
Add a pinning UX: installer flag + wizard toggle to use tagged releases or commit SHAs.

## Scope
- install.sh: add --pin-ref or --confirm-ref flag that prints resolved SHA and optionally persists it.
- Wizard: toggle for pinned install and copy command with ACFS_REF set.

## Testing
- Unit test for ref resolution output.
- Web E2E for toggle + command generation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can easily pin installs without manual edits.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:32:57.446034043Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:32:57.446034043Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.8","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:32:57.446034043Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

+acfs doctor --fix        # Apply safe fixes for failed checks
+acfs doctor --dry-run    # Preview fixes without applying
+acfs doctor --no-cache   # Skip cache, run all checks fresh
 ```

+### Auto-Fix Mode (`--fix`)
+
+The `--fix` flag automatically applies safe, deterministic fixes for common issues:
+
+```bash
+acfs doctor --fix             # Apply safe fixes
+acfs doctor --fix --dry-run   # Preview fixes without applying
+```
+
+#### Safe Auto-Fixers
+
+These fixes are applied automatically when `--fix` is used:
+
+| Fix ID | Description | Undo Strategy |
+|--------|-------------|---------------|
+| `fix.path.ordering` | Prepend ACFS directories to PATH in .zshrc | Restore backup |
+| `fix.config.copy` | Copy missing ~/.acfs config files | Remove copied file |
+| `fix.dcg.hook` | Install DCG pre-tool-use hook | Run `dcg uninstall` |
+| `fix.symlink.create` | Create missing tool symlinks | Remove symlink |
+| `fix.plugin.clone` | Clone missing zsh plugins | Remove cloned directory |
+| `fix.acfs.sourcing` | Add ACFS sourcing to .zshrc | Restore backup |
+
+#### Safety Guarantees
+
+- **Never deletes user files** — Only creates, modifies, or symlinks
+- **Backups before modify** — SHA256-verified backups of all modified files
+- **Idempotent** — Safe to run multiple times
+- **Logged** — All changes recorded to `~/.local/share/acfs/doctor.log`
+- **Reversible** — Every fix has an undo command
+
+#### Example Dry-Run Output
+
+```
+DRY-RUN: acfs doctor --fix
+
+Would apply the following fixes:
+
+  [fix.path.ordering]
+    Action: Prepend PATH directories to ~/.zshrc
+    File: ~/.zshrc
+    Backup: Yes (SHA256 verified)
+
+  [fix.acfs.sourcing]
+    Action: Add ACFS sourcing to .zshrc
+    File: ~/.zshrc
+    Backup: Yes (SHA256 verified)
+
+Fixes that require manual action:
+  [shell.ohmyzsh]
+    Status: FAIL
+    Suggestion: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh | bash
+
+Summary: 2 auto-fixes, 0 prompted, 1 manual
+```
+
+#### Manual-Only Fixes
+
+Some operations are never auto-fixed and instead provide suggestions:
+
+- Package manager operations (`apt install ...`)
+- Anything requiring sudo
+- File deletions
+- Complex shell configuration changes
+
+#### Undoing Changes
+
+All changes made by `--fix` can be undone:
+
+```bash
+acfs undo --list      # List all changes
+acfs undo chg_0001    # Undo specific change
+acfs undo --all       # Undo all changes from last session
+```
+
